summary:
The patient likely suffers from Phenylketonuria based on symptoms including blue eyes, blond hair, fair skin, and a positive Guthrie test indicative of elevated blood levels of phenylalanine leading to potential neurological damage unless managed properly through dietary restrictions or other interventions such as L-Dopa supplementation where indicated clinically after thorough evaluation.

The incorrect choice among those listed appears related to specific physical manifestations not typically described under standard presentations of classic PKU; hence it would most logically exclude description about white patch formation rather than others mentioned accurately reflecting known aspects associated with undiagnosed/unmanaged cases of PKU per current medical literature reviewed here focusing primarily upon core diagnostic features alongside common complications thereof when unaddressed over time affecting growth/developmental milestones adversely impacting quality life significantly otherwise preventably mitigated early diagnosis-wise ideally followed up closely thereafter throughout childhood into adulthood requiring lifelong monitoring adjustments accordingly tailored individually depending severity specifics etcetera...
